headline: Autonomous agent loops lead the month as recursive self-improvement breaks out across coding tooling.
body: The agent loop holds the leading position and recursive self-improvement breaks out alongside it, anchored by self-correcting coding workflows like Codex's Ralph loop and self-improving knowledge bases built on Hermes and Obsidian. Function calling and model capability also lead, while human-AI collaboration weakens as machine-mediated activity grows, with bots now outnumbering humans in web requests and AI agents beginning to browse and transact. RAG, test-time behavior, and project management enter as new breakouts, reflecting the shift toward persistent memory and context engineering over raw prompting. On the model side, OpenRouter's Fusion ensemble drew claims of Fable-level performance through a judge model but underperformed single strong models on practical coding and math tasks.
